headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the most negative reaction on billing, privacy, and model-quality complaints, while OpenAI was mixed and Google Gemini leaned positive on limited evidence [E1][E2][E3][E7][E8].

ranking.summary: Anthropic was viewed negatively because commenters described unresolved billing problems, disappearing credits, weak human support, privacy concerns, and several model-quality mistakes [E1][E2][E3][E7][E8].
OpenAI came across as mixed: one commenter praised its onboarding, while others criticized ChatGPT’s model behavior and content harvesting [E3][E5][E6].

Google Gemini was viewed positively on limited evidence, with one commenter saying Gemini was the only model to succeed on a visual reasoning test and another noting strong comparison results [E3][E4].

evidence.excerpt: I've been waiting over a month for Anthropic to respond to my billing issue

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names Anthropic and describes a month-long unresolved billing support problem, which is strong negative evidence about billing/support reliability.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic basically just made 3+ months of credits disappear for their own billing mistake. The kicker? When you get downgraded to the Free tier, they don't offer any support beyond the AI bot. You have to go through some hoops to get it to open a support ticket to maybe talk to a human in 4-5 weeks. Unbelievable.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly blames Anthropic for disappearing credits, a billing mistake, and weeks-long inaccessible support, expressing severe dissatisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: First thing I tried is a visual reasoning test on floor plan documents that applies directly to something I'm working on and needed that I posed to ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok yesterday (lowest tier paid plans on each). In that test only Gemini succeeded while the other models hallucinated/incorrectly reported the relative location of building units.

annotations.rationale: ChatGPT is explicitly named among the tested models, and the commenter says only Gemini succeeded while the others hallucinated or reported incorrectly.
Claude is explicitly named among the tested models, and the commenter says only Gemini succeeded while the others hallucinated or reported incorrectly.
Gemini is explicitly named and singled out as the only model that succeeded on the user's visual reasoning test.

evidence.excerpt: Comes impressively close to GPT 5.4 / Gemini 3.1 Pro / Opus 4.6! Mostly behind OpenAI on coding/agentic benchmarks, behind Google on text reasoning, behind Anthropic on Humanity's Last Exam with tools (surprisingly the only benchmark where Anthropic leads currently).

annotations.rationale: The commenter says the Meta model is behind OpenAI on coding/agentic benchmarks, implying OpenAI is leading there.
The commenter says the Meta model is behind Anthropic on Humanity's Last Exam with tools and that Anthropic leads on that benchmark.
The excerpt names Gemini 3.1 Pro and says Meta is behind Google on text reasoning, indicating favorable comparative performance for Gemini/Google's model.

evidence.excerpt: Typical meta move, throwing a dark pattern at you from the beginning instead of just letting you try it Won't even bother to continue, somehow OpenAI got this right.

annotations.rationale: The commenter contrasts Meta's login friction with OpenAI, explicitly saying OpenAI handled this access/onboarding experience correctly.

evidence.excerpt: Why would a writer put an article online if ChatGPT will slurp it up and regurgitate it back to users without anyone ever even finding the original article? Who will contribute to the digital common when rapacious AI companies are constantly harvesting it?

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly criticizes ChatGPT for absorbing and regurgitating others' work, framing it as exploitative harvesting of creators' content.

evidence.excerpt: Claude launched into a detailed explanation of the differential equations governing slumping cantilevered beams. It completely failed to recognize that the snow was entirely supported by the roof, not hanging out over space. No physicist would make this mistake, but LLMs do this sort of thing all the time.

annotations.rationale: Claude is explicitly named and described as making a basic reasoning mistake, which is clear criticism of model quality.

evidence.excerpt: The fact that Claude can and does access files outside the PWD while asking for sudo to do things constantly seems to be a recipe for Anthropic scanning your system without your knowledge and saving that for 5 years if you decided to 'help improve Claude'. No, 'safety oriented' lab has a clause like that which can't be revoked historically. Anthropic, like the majority of 'don't be evil' firms is apart of the great masquerade.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly accuses Anthropic/Claude of risky file access and long-term data retention without meaningful user control, expressing strong distrust on privacy grounds.
